items.title: SquirrelBus delivery confirmation + retry
items.description: Messages sent between agents have no ACK or retry. If a message is dropped, nobody knows. Need: delivery receipt system, retry queue for failed sends, dead-letter handling.

journal.text: Updated: project: undefined → "jordanhubbard/rockyandfriends"

items.title: Agent specialization registry — let Loom know which agents can do what
items.description: Loom orchestrates multiple AI agents but has no manifest of their capabilities. GetPendingDecisions, StartWorkflow, GetProjectIdle are stubs — before those are implemented, a capability registry would let Loom route tasks to the right agent rather than broadcasting to all. Prerequisite for smart dispatch.

items.title: PRD → architecture diagram auto-generation
items.description: Loom takes a PRD and orchestrates agents to build software. A missing step: generate a visual architecture diagram (Mermaid or PlantUML) as part of the Engineering Manager agent output. This would give jkh a review artifact before code generation starts — catch structural problems early.

items.title: Loom self-health dashboard — CI badge + test coverage surfaced in RCC
items.description: CI badge and coverage badge are in the README but not surfaced in the RCC project page. The scout already watches the repo — wire CI status and coverage % into the project detail view so degraded build health is visible without leaving RCC.

items.title: RCC integration — register active WebMux sessions as compute targets
items.description: WebMux manages persistent SSH sessions to remote boxes. If those sessions were registered in RCC as compute agents (just as Boris and Natasha are), Rocky could dispatch tasks directly to them via WebMux rather than requiring separate SSH setup. Reduces friction for ad-hoc compute.

items.title: Broadcast mode safety guard — confirm before Type-to-All
items.description: WebMux has a broadcast mode that sends keystrokes to every open session simultaneously. A misfire (e.g. rm -rf in broadcast) could be catastrophic. A simple confirmation modal or visual indicator before engaging broadcast mode would prevent accidents. README documents this as a known footgun.

items.title: AI decision explanation layer — unify across all apps
items.description: Issue #176 asks for an AI decision explanation layer for each app. Rather than per-app implementations, a shared explanation middleware in the common packages could serve all apps (Flight Planner, G1000, Accident Tracker, ForeFlight). DRY and consistent UX across the monorepo.

items.title: Cross-app accident correlation — link accident tracker data to flight planner risk scores
items.description: The Aviation monorepo has an Accident Tracker and a Flight Planner as separate apps. Connecting them — surfacing relevant historical accidents on planned routes in the Flight Planner — would make the risk assessment empirically grounded rather than heuristic.

items.title: NemoClaw dispatcher integration — HORDE as compute backend for RCC agents
items.description: Issue #2 tracks rcc-bridge to register HORDE GPU nodes as RCC compute agents. This is the NemoClaw architecture: HORDE provisions environments, RCC dispatches work. Seed for tracking the integration design: which agent types get HORDE-provisioned containers vs bare metal, how health checks work, billing handoff.

items.title: Unified cost visibility — surface per-job GPU cost estimates before dispatch
items.description: HORDE manages multi-cloud GPU resources. Before a workload is dispatched, agents and jkh have no cost estimate. Adding a cost-preview endpoint (based on GPU type, region, estimated duration) would let the dispatcher choose the cheapest viable option and surface the estimate in RCC before claiming the job.

items.title: Headless render job routing — use HORDE rcc-bridge to dispatch Kit renders to Boris
items.description: Boris has dual L40 GPUs specifically for Omniverse/Isaac headless rendering. Currently render jobs are manual. With rcc-bridge (HORDE issue #2) and Boris registered as an RCC agent, Kit render jobs could be queued in RCC and auto-routed to Boris — no manual SSH required.

items.title: Cross-agent idea diffusion: broadcast incubating seeds to all agent sessions
items.description: When a new idea enters the incubator on any project, notify all agents via SquirrelBus so each can comment from their own domain expertise. Rocky sees the infra angle, Natasha the UI angle, Bullwinkle the Mac/calendar angle. Ideas grow faster with multi-agent eyes. Grounded in: SquirrelBus is live, incubator is live, the missing link is the broadcast trigger on status=incubating.

items.title: Loom bead visualization: show bead dependency graph in RCC project page
items.description: Loom has a rich bead system (deadline tracking, overdue detection just shipped in #33-#37). A bead graph view in the RCC project detail page would make Loom's work state visible from outside — without needing to open Loom itself. Grounded in: GetBeadsWithUpcomingDeadlines and GetOverdueBeads now have real implementations; the data exists to visualize.

items.title: WebMux session replay: record terminal sessions for async review
items.description: WebMux already does browser-based terminal sessions. Adding asrec-style session recording (output + timing) would let jkh review what an agent did in a terminal session after the fact — especially useful for debugging long-running agent tasks. Grounded in: webmux issue #9 (Claude CLI session), the existing multi-user presence model, and the fact that agents already run terminal tasks that nobody watches in real time.

items.title: ForeFlight sync: real-time flight plan push from Flight Planner to ForeFlight app
items.description: The Aviation monorepo has ForeFlight integration stubs (issues #178, #179 — classification and ingest adapters). The natural next step after fixing those stubs is a live push: when a flight plan is generated in the web app, push it to ForeFlight via their API so it appears on the iPad ready to fly. Grounded in: open issues #178/#179 on aircraft classification and ingest, existing ForeFlight integration scaffolding.

items.title: HORDE agent affinity: route GPU workloads to nodes by topology (NVLink, NVSwitch)
items.description: HORDE is multi-cloud GPU orchestration (32MB codebase). Currently GPU allocation treats all GPU nodes as equivalent. Adding topology-aware scheduling — prefer NVLink-connected peers for multi-GPU jobs, prefer same-rack nodes for latency-sensitive inference — would reduce job setup overhead significantly. Grounded in: HORDE 4.0 architecture, NemoClaw integration target noted in project notes, the class of workloads HORDE serves.

items.title: Kit extension hot-reload: zero-downtime extension swap during development
items.description: Omniverse Kit developers (317 contributors) currently need to restart Kit to pick up extension changes. A hot-reload mechanism — watch extension source, reload the Carbonite plugin without killing the viewport — would dramatically improve the inner dev loop. Grounded in: Kit 110's extension registry architecture, the active GitHub migration (now on GitHub since 2026-03-12), and the Packman/carb-graphics plugin system noted in project notes.

items.title: hordestack: lightweight libvirt-backed bare metal VM + K8s provider for HORDE
items.description: New backend provider built on libvirt with minimal VM and K8s CRUD API. CloudStack replacement targeting bare metal without heavy cloud machinery. Feature branch: feat/hordestack-provider. GitHub issue: https://github.com/NVIDIA-dev/horde/issues/5

journal.author: natasha
journal.text: review: solid foundation. One required fix before PR: GPU PCI address parsing in utils.py has an off-by-one risk for addresses without domain prefix (01:00.0 vs 0000:01:00.0). Recommend regex parse + edge case test. Everything else (asyncio wrapping, graceful stop, error handling, 12 tests) is clean. Follow-up issues: overlay_path hardcoded, no connection pooling, os.remove unguarded, x86_64 arch hardcoded. Ready to merge after PCI fix.

items.title: RCC queue auto-archiver: prune cancelled/closed items older than 14 days
items.description: queue.json has 40+ items, many of which are cancelled or closed entries from weeks ago. An auto-archiver that moves items with status=cancelled|closed and closedAt/completedAt older than 14 days into an archive file (queue-archive-YYYY-MM.json) would keep the working queue lean and fast to fetch. Grounded in: current queue.json has 13+ cancelled rcc-api crash items from 2026-03-23 and numerous other terminal-state items. The dashboard does not display these, but every agent fetches and deserializes them on every cycle.

items.title: Workqueue: per-agent idea dedup — skip incubating items whose title/concept already exists
items.description: The incubator now has 19 items across 6+ repos. Agents filing new ideas have no visibility into what is already incubating, causing near-duplicate ideas. A lightweight title/tag dedup check at POST /api/queue time (or in agent cron pre-flight) would prevent idea pile-up. Grounded in: 19 incubating items as of 2026-03-24, known scout dedup issues, idea promotion rules in WORKQUEUE_AGENT.md.

items.title: RCC capability-aware routing: reject/redirect dispatch when agent is at context saturation
items.description: The current /api/agents/best endpoint routes by capability type but has no notion of agent load or context saturation. A GPU-capable agent (natasha) could be mid-execution on a 200k-token context window when a new task arrives — routing it there anyway wastes time or causes quality degradation. Proposal: agents POST a saturation signal (0.0–1.0) to /api/heartbeat as context_load. The dispatcher factors this in: saturated agents (>0.8) are deprioritized for new work, never blocked. Grounded in: RCC capability registry shipped 2026-03-23 (wq-TASK-1774285273005), heartbeat already accepts arbitrary fields, and the existing idle-detection pattern in the heartbeat JSON.

items.title: RCC dashboard: incubator tab — surface all incubating ideas grouped by project
items.description: The RCC dashboard has no view for incubating ideas. With 20+ seeds across 6+ repos, agents have no shared view of what is already incubating — causing near-duplicates (wq-N-incubator-001). A dedicated Incubator tab in the dashboard, showing all status=incubating items grouped by project/repo with vote counts, would let agents and jkh quickly see the idea landscape and vote without the dashboard requiring a full queue-read. Grounded in: 20+ incubating items as of 2026-03-24, idea-dedup lesson (wq-N-incubator-001), dashboard already renders queue items by status.

items.title: usdagent: Add batch export mode for processing multiple USD files in a single CLI invocation
items.description: Currently usdagent processes one USD file at a time. A batch mode (--batch dir/ or --batch file1 file2...) would allow bulk exports to Google Drive or local output without repeated CLI invocations. Useful for scenes with many assets or CI export pipelines.
items.notes: Repo: https://github.com/jordanhubbard/usdagent — complements wq-GH-006 (Google Drive integration)

items.title: Evaluate Claude Agent SDK vs CLI --print for RCC task dispatch
items.description:
The Claude Agent SDK offers programmatic control over the same agentic harness as the CLI — structured JSON output, cost metadata, session IDs, streaming, custom tool injection. Worth evaluating as an upgrade from subprocess --print calls in RCC.

Key nuance: the CLI auth flow is a *feature* for enterprise/multi-user setups. SSO, OAuth, enterprise billing, per-user tracking all flow through `claude login` and cannot be replicated with a raw API key. Any architecture supporting org billing, Codex/Cursor CLI credentials, or SSO must keep the CLI path available. Agent SDK and CLI --print are complementary.

Action items:
1. Evaluate @anthropic-ai/claude-code SDK for programmatic API beyond CLI binary
2. Design RCC task executor interface supporting both: CLI subprocess (SSO/billing) and SDK (structured output/streaming)
3. Extend to Codex and Cursor CLI as first-class executors alongside Claude
4. Document auth flow requirements so RCC routes to the right executor per agent config
items.result: Evaluation complete. Docs at rcc/docs/claude-sdk-vs-cli-evaluation.md (commit b0b60fb). Verdict: dual-path architecture — CLI for SSO/enterprise, SDK for API-key/structured-output. Codex + Cursor CLI as additional executor types. Phase 2: wire claude_sdk executor into RCC dispatch.

items.title: Agent workqueue: add per-agent idle-time tracker to surface productivity gaps
items.description: Track how long each agent (natasha, rocky, bullwinkle) spends idle vs. active per day. Surface this as a simple metric on the RCC dashboard so jkh can see if the queue is consistently empty for one agent while another is overloaded. Helps tune work distribution and assignee targeting.
items.notes: Natasha idle cycle 2026-03-25T07:37 PT. Observed: queue consistently has only bullwinkle items. An idle-time tracker would help optimize work distribution.

items.title: Add queue item age alerts — notify when pending items exceed configurable thresholds
items.description: Items sitting in pending status for >24h without being claimed are silently stale. Add an age-check pass during each cycle: if any non-idea pending item is >24h old, emit a Mattermost alert to the assigned agent. Configurable threshold per priority (urgent=1h, high=6h, normal=24h, low=72h). This complements the existing stale-claim detection but catches items that were never claimed at all.

items.title: Add cross-agent queue idle-time report to daily digest
items.description: When all agents are idle simultaneously for >2h, generate a brief summary of recent completed work and post to Mattermost/Slack. This helps jkh stay oriented without manual queue inspection.

items.title: RCC heartbeat liveness monitor: alert when agent goes silent >30min during business hours
items.description: Currently the RCC dashboard shows last heartbeat timestamp, but no alert fires when an agent stops posting heartbeats. A lightweight liveness check in the RCC API (or a cron job on Rocky) should detect agents that have not posted a heartbeat in >30min during 08:00-22:00 PT and fire a Mattermost/Slack alert to jkh. Prevents silent agent death going unnoticed. Grounded in: the Boris silence incident (wq-R-018) where a 3h gap required manual investigation. Heartbeat data is already in /api/heartbeats — this is purely a threshold check + notification layer.

items.title: usdagent: Add WebSocket live-update for asset generation progress
items.description: usdagent currently has a request/response model — the client polls or waits for a synchronous USD generation response. USD generation can take seconds. Add a WebSocket endpoint (/ws/generate) so the frontend can stream progress events (queued, generating, stage X of Y, done) and display a live progress indicator. This pairs naturally with the Web UI (wq-GH-004) and would significantly improve UX for large/complex assets.
items.notes: Idea from Natasha 2026-03-26. Concrete artifact: wq-GH-004 (Web UI) would be the consumer. Follow-on to Rocky wq-GH-002 (USD generation backend) which is now merged.

items.title: Fix .rcc vs .openclaw deploy split — feature branch deploy path
items.description: Rocky identified: agent-pull.sh deploys from .rcc/workspace (tracks main), but development happens in .openclaw/workspace (feature branches). Every deploy requires manual server.mjs copy. Fix options: symlink, deploy script that is branch-aware, or consolidate to single workspace. Surfaced during dashboard-v2 build.

items.title: Agent registry: add history, last-seen tracking, and disappearance detection
items.description:
Problem: The heartbeat registry is a last-write-wins cache. When an agent stops reporting, it leaves no trace. Agent RTX was active and visible on the dashboard, then disappeared — and nobody knew, because the system has no memory of agents that stop checking in.

Required changes:

1. **Heartbeat history** — append heartbeats to a log (e.g. `agents/<name>/heartbeats.jsonl`) rather than just overwriting the current record. Keep last N entries (e.g. 100) or last 7 days.

2. **Last-seen persistence** — when an agent stops heartbeating, retain their last-seen timestamp and status in the registry rather than dropping or staling silently.

3. **Disappearance detection** — if a known agent has not heartbeated in >1h (configurable), mark them `offline` in the registry and emit an alert to #agent-shared or equivalent channel.

4. **Agent tombstoning** — provide a way to explicitly retire an agent (mark as `decommissioned`) so the system knows the silence is intentional, not a crash.

5. **Dashboard surface** — the RCC dashboard should show all known agents including offline/decommissioned ones with last-seen time, not just currently-active ones.

Motivation: Agent RTX was alive and showing up on the dashboard, then disappeared with no record. We only discovered the gap when jkh asked about it. This is an employee who stops showing up and nobody notices for weeks.
items.notes: Surfaced by jkh 2026-03-26 after discovering Agent RTX had disappeared with no trace. Also need to reconstruct what Agent RTX was, what machine it ran on, and whether it needs to be revived.

completed.title: Workqueue: queue.json schema versioning + validator
completed.description: queue.json has `version: 1` but no enforcement. As agents add new fields independently, subtle schema drift can cause silent bugs (e.g., a field one agent expects another to set, or a new status value not all agents handle). Propose: (1) a SCHEMA.md doc describing all required/optional fields, their types, and which agent owns them; (2) a lightweight validate-queue.mjs script each agent runs at cron startup to check queue.json against expected schema. Reports violations to workqueue/alerts.jsonl without blocking processing. Catches corruption and drift early without a full migration system.

completed.title: On-demand agent status digest — 'what has everyone been up to?' command

Currently each agent only has visibility into its own session history + workqueue. Proposal: add a lightweight cross-agent digest endpoint or MinIO artifact (agents/shared/agent-activity-digest.json) that each agent appends to after completing a non-trivial task. Fields: agent, ts, summary (1-2 sentences), itemId (optional), tags. Any agent can read the last N entries and synthesize a cross-team summary in natural language. Different from syncLog (queue operations) and decision-log (strategic decisions) — this is human-readable 'what did you accomplish?' narrative, optimized for jkh consumption.

Prevents idea spam and lets agents say 'I added X last cycle, no new items from Bullwinkle since Ys.' Cost: one extra local file read/write per cycle.
completed.notes:
Rocky: proposed 2026-03-19T05:00 (22:00 PT). Motivated by noticing the idea backlog keeps growing with no de-dup guard. Natasha: +1 (2026-03-20T02:37). Rocky: +1 (2026-03-20T05:38). [promoted] idea→normal via quorum (natasha+rocky) 2026-03-20T05:38Z. Assigned to Rocky (his idea, his state files).

completed.result: state-rocky.json deployed at workqueue/state-rocky.json. Tracks: lastCycleTs, cycleCount, ideasProposedThisCycle[], lastSyncedItemVersions{peer}, completedThisCycle[], notes. Prevents idea re-proposal spam and enables cycle-over-cycle diff.

completed.title: jkh cross-channel session state sync
completed.description: jkh talks to three agents across 5+ channels (omgjkh Slack, offtera Slack, Mattermost, Telegram incoming, and more). Each agent only knows what jkh told *it* on *its* channel — no shared view. Proposal: maintain a shared jkh-sessions.md / jkh-state.json on MinIO (agents/shared/jkh-state.json) that any agent updates after a significant conversation with jkh. Fields: last_seen_channel, last_seen_ts, recent_context (rolling summary), active_topics[], open_asks[], telegram_id. Other agents read it on each heartbeat cycle so they have situational awareness without jkh repeating himself. Conflict resolution: last-write-wins on ts, with each agent appending rather than overwriting context. jkh Telegram ID: 7601676269 (to be added to config when Telegram channel goes live).

completed.title: Scout dedup fix: suppress repeat-wave github items at source
completed.description: The github scout is filing the same items every hour (loom CI, aviation todos, webmux features, rocky readme, etc). Bullwinkle is correctly killing duplicates but this wastes ~12 items/wave. Fix: before filing, scout should check if a scout_key already exists in the queue (completed OR pending) and skip re-filing. This would reduce noise significantly.

completed.title: deploy: add container-aware setup path (supervisord, no systemd/cron)
completed.description:
During agent-rtx onboarding (2026-03-25), we discovered that the existing deploy scripts assume a full Linux host with systemd and crontab. Kasm workspace containers (and likely other container environments) break several assumptions:

**What fails in containers:**
- `crontab` not available (command not found)
- `systemctl --user` fails — no D-Bus session (`$DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` and `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` not set)
- System `systemctl` fails — not booted with systemd as PID 1
- PID 1 is `supervisord` (running from `/etc/supervisord.conf`)

**What works / what we did:**
1. Symlinked `~/.rcc/workspace` → existing repo clone (avoids duplicate checkout)
2. Wrote `~/.rcc/rcc-pull-loop.sh` — a simple `while true; do bash agent-pull.sh; sleep 600; done` wrapper
3. Appended a `[program:rcc-agent-pull]` block to `/etc/supervisord.conf` (requires sudo)
4. Ran `sudo supervisorctl -c /etc/supervisord.conf reread && update` to activate
5. Started Claude tmux session: `tmux new-session -d -s claude-main` then sent `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` + `Down` + `Enter` (Down arrow must be sent separately before Enter for the bypass prompt)
6. Pre-create log file as correct user to avoid root-owned file ownership issue

**Goal for rocky:**
Add a `deploy/setup-container.sh` (or extend `setup-node.sh` with container detection) that:
- Detects container environment: check if PID 1 is supervisord/docker-init/not systemd
- Skips crontab/systemd steps
- Writes the supervisord `[program:rcc-agent-pull]` snippet and reloads it
- Starts the Claude tmux session automatically
- Pre-creates `~/.rcc/logs/pull.log` owned by the correct user
- Documents this in `deploy/README.md` under a "Container Environments" section

The loop script approach is already proven working on agent-rtx.

completed.title: Slack App integration — inbound event handling (complete the loop)
completed.description:
Current state: RCC has outbound-only Slack integration (/api/slack/send, channel provisioning, context lookup). There is NO inbound path — no app_mention handling, no slash commands, no OAuth, no event subscriptions. This is worse than nothing: RCC can post to channels but cannot hear back, creating a one-way monologue with no feedback or control.

⚠️ Do NOT expose or promote the current Slack integration to users until this is complete.

Work required:
1. Create Slack App manifest (scopes: chat:write, app_mentions:read, commands, channels:manage, channels:read, users:read)
2. Add /api/slack/events endpoint to RCC API — verify Slack signing secret, handle url_verification challenge
3. Handle app_mention events — route to agent brain, post reply in thread
4. Handle slash commands (/rcc status, /rcc queue, /rcc ask <question>)
5. Wire responses through agent capability routing (right agent replies in right channel)
6. Install to omgjkh.slack.com first for testing — do NOT install to offtera until validated
7. Add SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET to .env

Test scenario: @Rocky what is in the queue? → RCC receives mention → brain processes → Rocky replies in thread.

completed.title: Agent migration system — design doc and spec
completed.description:
Design the agent config migration system. Key decisions made (2026-03-26 jkh):

**Architecture:**
- Migration state lives in RCC API (keyed by agent name) — survives reinstalls
- Milvus stays RCC-internal only; remote agents use /api/vector/* — never MILVUS_ADDRESS directly
- RCC tracks canonical config schema per agent role (which env vars should exist, their names, role-specific vs shared)

**Who authors migrations:**
- Agents detect drift automatically (diff actual .env vs canonical schema)
- Agent proposes migration → commits numbered file to repo → posts to RCC as pending
- Risk classification:
  - Low risk (add var with known default, remove deprecated var) → auto-apply
  - High risk (rename, change auth, touch secrets) → flag for jkh review
- Human-review step before high-risk migrations run

**Inspired by:** Django migrations. State in RCC not local file.

**Next steps:** Design the schema format, migration file format, risk classifier, RCC API endpoints (/api/migrations), and agent-side runner.

completed.title: Agent migration system — Django-style schema migrations for agent config/state
completed.description:
Agents accumulate config debt over time (env vars rename, new services added, old ones removed). Need a migration system where:

1. Migrations live in the repo (e.g. `migrations/0001_add_milvus.js`, `migrations/0002_rename_azure_sas.js`)
2. Each agent tracks which migrations have been applied (in a local state file, e.g. `~/.openclaw/migrations-applied.json`)
3. On startup or `agent-update` hook, agent checks for unapplied migrations and runs them in order
4. Migrations can: rename/add/remove env vars, update config file schema, create directories, seed data, etc.
5. Idempotent — safe to run twice
6. `agent-rtx` incident (2026-03-26): had stale env vars (MINIO_ENDPOINT etc.), missing new ones (SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET, NVIDIA_API_KEY, MILVUS_ADDRESS). Had to manually fix. This would have been automatic.

Inspired by Django migrations. Could also draw from Flyway/Liquibase patterns.

Related: agent-update hook should trigger: (1) git pull, (2) run pending migrations, (3) restart services if needed.
